title: T-020 — API: Technical lab-results read model + Quality write bridge
prompt:
# T-020 — API: Technical lab-results read model + Quality write bridge

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.5, §10.6, §10.8
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Technical read model for Quality-owned lab results; writes only through Quality bridge.

Ownership red-line: lab_results are Quality-owned. Technical may consume/read and request/write only through a Quality permission/service bridge if present. Do not model Technical as canonical authoring owner. If bridge is missing, make POST unavailable with explicit blocker rather than silently creating Technical-owned rows.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/lab-results/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/lab/read-model.ts [create]
- lib/technical/lab/quality-bridge-client.ts [create]
- tests/api/lab-results.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- WO close gate logic (08-PRODUCTION)
- UI Lab Results Log (T-067 docs gap)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given GET ?test_type=allergen_elisa&result_status=fail, then Technical returns only matching Quality-owned read-model rows.
2. Given Technical POST is called and no Quality bridge exists, then request fails with explicit QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING/501 and no lab_results row is written by Technical.
3. Given Quality bridge exists and caller has required Quality permission, then Technical bridge call delegates write and readback shows the Quality-owned row.
4. Given test_type=atp_swab result_status/threshold fields are present, then Technical displays/returns the Quality-calculated status without recalculating ownership locally.

## Test strategy
- RED tests covering V-TEC-44 trigger semantics.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/lab-results.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not call external lab APIs (Phase 2).
- Do not write lab_results directly from Technical-owned service/table code.
- Do not model lab_results as Technical-owned canonical authoring; treat as Quality-owned read model/bridge for Phase 1.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
